go_no_go_recommendation: Proceed with Caution. The project addresses a high-risk scenario with potentially catastrophic consequences, and the initial plan has several critical gaps. The immediate actions outlined above are essential to mitigate the most pressing risks related to evacuation, communication, and jurisdictional clarity. However, the plan needs to be strengthened by addressing long-term recovery, cybersecurity risks, and the specific needs of vulnerable populations, as highlighted in the 'Review conclusion' section of the 'assumptions.md' file. Proceeding without addressing these gaps would significantly increase the risk of failure and potential loss of life. Therefore, the project should proceed only if these immediate actions are taken and a plan is put in place to address the identified gaps within the next 72 hours.
combined_summary:
The three most critical, immediate actions are:

1.  **Establish Contraflow Traffic Control Immediately:** Deploy personnel to set up contraflow lanes on key routes (US-191, US-20, US-89) to maximize outbound traffic flow. This is essential to expedite evacuation and mitigate the risk of traffic bottlenecks.
2.  **Activate FEMA IPAWS Redundancy Immediately:** Activate FEMA IPAWS for public alerts and deploy National Guard signal corps with satellite communication equipment. This mitigates the risk of communication failure due to ashfall or seismic activity, ensuring the public receives timely information.
3.  **Define Authority Transfer Protocols Now:** Draft and circulate MOUs for authority transfer from NPS to State Governors to clarify jurisdictional responsibilities. This mitigates the risk of jurisdictional disputes and ensures a smooth transition of authority as evacuees cross park boundaries.

feedback.title: Establish Contraflow Traffic Control
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Immediately deploy National Park Service LE Rangers and Wyoming Highway Patrol to US-191, US-20, and US-89 by 2026-02-01 12:00 to begin setting up contraflow lanes. This is needed to maximize outbound traffic flow.
*   Establish communication channels between all deployed units using satellite phones by 2026-02-01 12:30, given the potential failure of cell towers. This ensures coordinated traffic management.
*   Implement a real-time traffic monitoring system using drone-based cameras along evacuation routes by 2026-02-01 13:00. This provides data for dynamic rerouting.
*   Designate staging areas every 50km along US-191, US-20, and US-89 by 2026-02-01 14:00 for disabled vehicles and emergency services. This prevents traffic blockages.

feedback.title: Mobilize Bottled Water Convoys
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Contact at least 3 bottled water suppliers in Salt Lake City by 2026-02-01 12:00 to confirm availability of at least 500,000 liters of bottled water. This ensures sufficient supply for evacuees.
*   Secure at least 20 transport trucks with drivers by 2026-02-01 13:00 to begin loading and dispatching water convoys from Salt Lake City towards Bozeman and Idaho Falls. This is needed to meet the 12-hour delivery target.
*   Establish a GPS tracking system for all water convoys by 2026-02-01 13:30. This allows for real-time monitoring and rerouting if necessary.
*   Pre-arrange security escorts (National Guard) for convoys entering the evacuation zones by 2026-02-01 14:00. This protects the supplies from looting.

feedback.title: Pre-Stage Respiratory Protection
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Confirm the availability of at least 100,000 N95 respirators from suppliers by 2026-02-01 12:00. This ensures sufficient protection for the initial evacuees.
*   Dispatch at least 5 trucks to transport the respirators to pre-designated distribution points in West Yellowstone, Gardiner, Cody, Bozeman, and Idaho Falls by 2026-02-01 13:00. This ensures rapid distribution.
*   Establish a system for tracking respirator distribution, ensuring equitable access for all evacuees by 2026-02-01 14:00. This prevents shortages in specific areas.
*   Prepare instructional materials (printed and video) in multiple languages on the proper use of N95 respirators by 2026-02-01 15:00. This ensures effective use of the respirators.

feedback.title: Establish Mass Casualty Intake Centers
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Immediately confirm the availability of Bozeman, MT (Field Report) and Idaho Falls, ID (Bonneville HS) as mass casualty and refugee intake centers by 2026-02-01 12:00. This ensures adequate shelter capacity.
*   Deploy at least 2 medical teams (doctors, nurses, paramedics) to each intake center by 2026-02-01 13:00. This provides immediate medical support.
*   Equip each intake center with at least 500 cots, blankets, and hygiene kits by 2026-02-01 14:00. This ensures basic comfort for evacuees.
*   Establish a registration system at each intake center to track evacuees and their needs by 2026-02-01 15:00. This facilitates resource allocation and family reunification.

feedback.title: Activate FEMA IPAWS Redundancy
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Immediately activate FEMA IPAWS for emergency broadcasting by 2026-02-01 12:00. This is needed to provide immediate alerts to the public.
*   Deploy National Guard signal corps with satellite communication equipment to West Yellowstone, Gardiner, and Cody by 2026-02-01 13:00. This provides comms bridging in case of cell tower failure.
*   Establish a backup communication channel using amateur radio operators by 2026-02-01 14:00. This ensures redundancy in communication.
*   Test all communication channels (IPAWS, satellite, radio) by 2026-02-01 15:00 to confirm functionality. This verifies communication effectiveness.

feedback.title: Define Authority Transfer Protocols
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Immediately draft Memoranda of Understanding (MOUs) for authority transfer from NPS to State Governors by 2026-02-01 12:00. This clarifies jurisdictional responsibilities.
*   Circulate the draft MOUs to the NPS and the Governors of Wyoming, Montana, and Idaho for immediate review and signature by 2026-02-01 18:00. This ensures legal clarity.
*   Establish a legal liaison team at the FEMA Region VIII RRCC in Denver by 2026-02-01 14:00 to address any legal challenges arising from the authority transfer. This provides immediate legal support.
*   Communicate the authority transfer protocols to all relevant agencies (NPS, State Patrol, National Guard) by 2026-02-01 20:00. This ensures operational understanding.

feedback.title: Establish Exclusion Zone Security
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Immediately deploy National Guard units to pre-designated exclusion zone perimeter points around Yellowstone National Park by 2026-02-01 12:00. This prevents unauthorized entry.
*   Establish a clear chain of command for the National Guard units, reporting directly to the Unified Command at the FEMA Region VIII RRCC by 2026-02-01 13:00. This ensures coordinated security.
*   Implement a system for monitoring the exclusion zone using drone-based surveillance by 2026-02-01 14:00. This provides real-time situational awareness.
*   Establish protocols for handling potential looting incidents, including rules of engagement and communication procedures by 2026-02-01 15:00. This ensures appropriate response to security breaches.

feedback.title: Establish VEI-7 Escalation Trigger
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Immediately define specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound (SMART) trigger points for escalating the evacuation zone to 500km based on USGS data by 2026-02-01 12:00. This ensures a data-driven decision.
*   Establish a communication protocol for immediately notifying the Governors of Wyoming, Montana, Idaho, Utah, and South Dakota upon reaching the VEI-7 trigger point by 2026-02-01 13:00. This ensures timely notification.
*   Prepare maps and evacuation plans for the expanded 500km zone, including routes, shelter locations, and resource allocation by 2026-02-01 14:00. This ensures preparedness for escalation.
*   Conduct a tabletop exercise simulating the VEI-7 escalation scenario with all relevant agencies by 2026-02-01 16:00. This validates the escalation plan.
